title: नरसिंह सहस्रनाम
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
उग्रं वीरं महाविष्णुं ज्वलन्तं सर्वतोमुखम्।
नृसिंहं भीषणं भद्रं मृत्युमृत्युं नमाम्यहम्॥

सत्यं सत्यं पुनः सत्यमुद्धृत्य भुजमुच्यते।
वेदशास्त्रात्परं नास्ति न देवं नरकेसरात्॥

॥ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥
नरसिंहो महासिंहो दिव्यसिंहो महाबलः।
उग्रसिंहः सर्वात्मा सर्वलोकनमस्कृतः॥ १॥

हिरण्यकशिपुध्वंसी प्रह्लादवरदः प्रभुः।
विष्णुर्जिष्णुर्महाविष्णुः स्तम्भोद्भवो नृकेसरी॥ २॥

दैत्यान्तको महाभीमो भयकृद्भयनाशनः।
ज्वालामुखो ज्वालमाली महाज्वालः प्रतापवान्॥ ३॥

महादंष्ट्रो महाकायो महावक्त्रो महानखः।
महारौद्रो महाशूरो महातेजाः प्रभाकरः॥ ४॥

विकटाट्टहासकारी च सर्वदैत्यविनाशनः।
क्रूरो विक्रमशीलश्च वज्रदेहो वज्रनखः॥ ५॥

योगानन्दो नारसिंहो नारायणपरात्परः।
परमात्मा परब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दविग्रहः॥ ६॥

लक्ष्मीनृसिंहो देवेशो ब्रह्मरुद्रादिवन्दितः।
सहस्रबाहुः सर्वज्ञः सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायकः॥ ७॥

सर्वभूतहिते रतो दीनानाथपरायणः।
भक्तवत्सलो दयालुश्च शरण्यः सर्वरक्षकः॥ ८॥

सर्वव्याधिविनाशी च सर्वरोगनिवारकः।
सर्वपापहरो देवो सर्वकल्मषनाशनः॥ ९॥

सर्वदारिद्र्यशमनो सर्वशोकनिवारकः।
सर्वभयहरो वीरो सर्वमंगलकारकः॥ १०॥

ऋग्वेदो यजुर्वेदो सामवेदोऽथर्वणस्तथा।
वेदान्तसारो वेदात्मा वेदवेद्यः सनातनः॥ ११॥

ब्रह्मणो हृदयं विष्णोः शिवस्यात्मा सदाशिवः।
त्रिमूर्तिस्त्रिगुणातीतो निर्गुणो गुणसागरः॥ १२॥

दिशां पतिर्दिशां नाथो दिशाशूलविभूषणः।
कालः कालविधाता च कालरूपी कलानिधिः॥ १३॥

सिंहनादो महानादो दिग्गजार्तिविनाशनः।
महाप्रभुर्महावीरो महादेवो महेश्वरः॥ १४॥

भक्तप्रियो भक्तजीवो भक्तरक्षणतत्परः।
पापघ्नो भवभीतिघ्नो दुष्टशिक्षणतत्परः॥ १५॥

सर्वलोकहिते रतः सर्वभूतपतिः परः।
क्षीरसागरशायी च शेषशायी गदाधरः॥ १६॥

शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मधारी चतुर्भुजो हरिः।
सुदर्शनधरो विष्णुः श्रीवत्साङ्कितवक्षकः॥ १७॥

पीताम्बरधरो देवो वनमालाविभूषितः।
कौस्तुभाभरणो श्रीमान् शार्ङ्गधन्वा जनार्दनः॥ १८॥

भूभारहारी देवेन्द्रो लोकनाथो जगत्पतिः।
श्रीनिवासो वैकुण्ठो मुकुन्दो मधुसूदनः॥ १९॥

गोविन्दो गोपतिः कृष्णो केशवो गरुडध्वजः।
वामनो जामदग्न्यश्च रामो रामोऽपराजितः॥ २०॥

कल्किर्विश्वम्भरः शौरिः अनन्तो वासुदेवकः।
प्रद्युम्नः पुरुषोत्तमो अनिरुद्धः सनातनः॥ २१॥

त्रिविक्रमो वामनश्च बलिबन्धनकारकः।
नरकान्तको मधुहा च पुण्यश्लोको जनप्रियः॥ २२॥

अव्ययो अक्षरो नित्यो अनन्तो अजरो अमरः।
सर्वगः सर्वभूतात्मा सर्वदृक् सर्वसाक्षिकः॥ २३॥

आदिदेवो महादेवो देवदेवो परात्परः।
सर्वाधारो निराधारो निराकारो निरञ्जनः॥ २४॥

सहस्रनामसम्पन्नो नृसिंहो भक्तवत्सलः।
भवभीतिहरो दाता सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकः॥ २५॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
सहस्रनामस्तोत्रं नरसिंहस्य यः पठेत्।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

सर्वभयविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वरोगविवर्जितः।
नृसिंहकृपया दिव्यं परमं पदमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री नरसिंह सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Ugram Viram Mahavishnum Jvalantam Sarvatomukham.
Nrisimham Bhishanam Bhadram Mrityumrityum Namamyaham.

Satyam Satyam Punah Satyam-uddhritya Bhujam-uchyate.
Veda-shastrat Param Nasti Na Devam Narakesarat.

|| Sahasranama Stotram ||
Narasimho Mahasimho Divyasimho Mahabalah.
Ugrasimhah Sarvatma Sarvaloka-namaskritah. || 1 ||

Hiranyakashipu-dhvamsi Prahlada-varadah Prabhuh.
Vishnur-Jishnur-Mahavishnuh Stambhodbhavo Nrikesari. || 2 ||

Daityantako Mahabhimo Bhayakrid-bhayanashanah.
Jwala-mukho Jwala-mali Mahajwalah Pratapavan. || 3 ||

Mahadamshtro Mahakayo Mahavaktro Mahanakhah.
Maharaudro Mahashuro Mahatejah Prabhakarah. || 4 ||

Vikatattahasa-kari Cha Sarva-daitya-vinashanah.
Kruro Vikrama-shilash-cha Vajra-deho Vajra-nakhah. || 5 ||

Yoganando Narasimho Narayana-paratparah.
Paramatma Parabrahma Sachchidananda-vigrahah. || 6 ||

Lakshmi-Nrisimho Devesho Brahma-rudradi-vanditah.
Sahasra-bahuh Sarvajnah Sarva-siddhi-pradayakah. || 7 ||

Sarva-bhuta-hite Rato Dinanatha-parayanah.
Bhakta-vatsalo Dayalush-cha Sharanyah Sarva-rakshakah. || 8 ||

Sarva-vyadhi-vinashi Cha Sarva-roga-nivarakah.
Sarva-papa-haro Devo Sarva-kalmasha-nashanah. || 9 ||

Sarva-daridrya-shamano Sarva-shoka-nivarakah.
Sarva-bhayaharo Viro Sarva-mangala-karakah. || 10 ||

Rigvedo Yajurvedo Samavedo Atharvanas-tatha.
Vedanta-saro Vedatma Veda-vedyah Sanatanah. || 11 ||

Brahmano Hridayam Vishnoh Shivasyatma Sadashivah.
Trimurti-strigunatito Nirguno Guna-sagarah. || 12 ||

Disham Patir-disham Natho Disha-shula-vibhushanah.
Kalah Kalavidhata Cha Kalarupi Kalanidhih. || 13 ||

Simha-nado Maha-nado Diggajaarti-vinashanah.
Mahaprabhu-Mahaviro Mahadevo Maheshwarah. || 14 ||

Bhakta-priyo Bhakta-jivo Bhakta-rakshana-tatparah.
Papa-ghno Bhava-bhiti-ghno Dushta-shikshana-tatparah. || 15 ||

Sarva-loka-hite Ratah Sarva-bhuta-patih Parah.
Kshira-sagara-shayi Cha Shesha-shayi Gadadharah. || 16 ||

Shankha-chakra-gada-padma-dhari Chaturbhujo Harih.
Sudarshana-dharo Vishnuh Shrivatsa-ankita-vakshakah. || 17 ||

Pitambara-dharo Devo Vanamala-vibhushitah.
Kaustubha-abharano Shriman Sharnga-dhanva Janardanah. || 18 ||

Bhubhara-hari Devendro Lokanatho Jagat-patih.
Shrinivaso Vaikuntho Mukundo Madhusudanah. || 19 ||

Govindo Gopatih Krishno Keshavo Garuda-dhvajah.
Vamano Jamadagnyash-cha Ramo Ramo Aparajitah. || 20 ||

Kalkir-vishvambharah Shaurih Ananto Vasudevakah.
Pradyumnah Purushottamo Aniruddhah Sanatanah. || 21 ||

Trivikramo Vamanash-cha Bali-bandhana-karakah.
Narakantako Madhuha Cha Punya-shloko Janapriyah. || 22 ||

Avyayo Aksharo Nityo Ananto Ajaro Amarah.
Sarvagah Sarva-bhutatma Sarvadrik Sarva-sakshikah. || 23 ||

Adidevo Mahadevo Devadevo Paratparah.
Sarvadharo Niradharo Nirakaro Niranjanah. || 24 ||

Sahasranama-sampanno Nrisimho Bhaktavatsalah.
Bhava-bhiti-haro Data Sarva-sampat-pradayakah. || 25 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Sahasranama-stotram Narasimhasya Yah Pathet.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnulokam Sa Gachchhati.

Sarva-bhaya-vinirmuktah Sarva-roga-vivarjitah.
Nrisimha-kripaya Divyam Paramam Padam-aapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Narasimha Sahasranama Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: नरसिंह सहस्रनाम में भगवान नरसिंह के सहस्र दिव्य नामों का वर्णन है। वे उग्र, वीर, महाविष्णु, ज्वलन्त, मृत्युमृत्यु (मृत्यु की भी मृत्यु) हैं। हिरण्यकशिपु का संहार कर प्रह्लाद को वरदान देने वाले, स्तम्भ से प्रकट हुए, महादंष्ट्र, महानख, सच्चिदानन्द विग्रह, लक्ष्मीनृसिंह, सर्वभूतहितरत, भक्तवत्सल, सर्वव्याधिविनाशी, सर्वमंगलकारक, परात्पर परमात्मा हैं। जो इस स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Reciting 1000 names of Narasimha destroys all sins
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, diseases, and calamities
Invokes divine protection equal to that of prahlada
Invokes the blessing of all siddhis and leads to vishnu loka
Most powerful prayer for protection against enemies and evil
description: Dedicated to Lord Narasimha, the Narasimha Sahasranama (नरसिंह सहस्रनाम) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Garuda Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to grant divine protection equal to that of Prahlada, remove all fears, diseases, and calamities, and bestow all siddhis and leads to Vishnu Loka. This sacred text is ideally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for enhanced blessings. The text contains 25 verses and takes around 25 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Sahasranama in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Sahasranama - 1000 names of Lord Narasimha in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Supreme prayer for protection and spiritual liberation.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Narasimha and what does this sahasranama invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha is the fourth avatar of Lord Vishnu — the man-lion incarnation who emerged from a pillar at twilight to slay the asura king Hiranyakashipu and protect his devotee, the boy-prince Prahlada. Hiranyakashipu had received a boon that he could not be killed by man or beast, indoors or outdoors, by day or by night, on earth or in sky, by any weapon — so Narasimha appeared as neither man nor beast, on the threshold, at twilight, on his lap, and tore the asura with his claws. The Narasimha Sahasranama invokes the Lord by 1000 names spanning his fierce protective form (Ugra, Pratapa, Bhima), his cosmic identity with Vishnu (Hari, Vasudeva, Govinda), his benevolent maternal aspect with Lakshmi (Lakshmi-priya, Yoga-rupa), and his liberative grace toward bhaktas. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7; Wikipedia Narasimha; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Narasimha Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Sahasranama is preserved in classical Vaishnavite tradition with the principal recension found in the Garuda Purana — one of the eighteen Mahapuranas — and parallel versions in the Brahmanda Purana, the Vamana Purana and the Ahirbudhnya Samhita of the Pancharatra Agama. The most widely circulated version today is the Garuda Purana recension. Sri Vaishnava sampradaya tradition preserves an additional Lakshmi-Narasimha Sahasranama associated with the Ahobilam Mutt, the principal Narasimha kshetra in Andhra Pradesh comprising nine shrines (Nava-Narasimha) including Bhargava Narasimha, Yogananda Narasimha, Chatravata Narasimha and Pavana Narasimha. (Sources: Garuda Purana; Hindupedia Narasimha; Wikipedia Ahobilam.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Narasimha Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the principal weekday for Narasimha worship, drawing on his role as the supreme remover of Saturn-related afflictions including Sade Sati and Dhaiya. Swati nakshatra is the special asterism associated with Narasimha (his birth nakshatra in some traditions). The supreme annual occasion is Narasimha Jayanti, falling on Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi (May), when devotees observe a fast and recite the sahasranama 1, 11, 21 or 108 times. Other peak occasions include all Ekadashis, especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi, the Brahmotsavam at Ahobilam, and the festival days at Simhachalam (Andhra Pradesh) and Mangalagiri. Devotees facing serious enemies, hostile environments, planetary affliction, or chronic disease are advised to recite the sahasranama daily. (Sources: Drikpanchang Narasimha Jayanti; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narasimha Sahasranama phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Narasimha Sahasranama promises sweeping rewards: comprehensive protection from all enemies (visible and invisible), destruction of negative entities including bhuta-preta-pishacha, freedom from black magic and abhichara, removal of Saturn-related afflictions including Sade Sati and Dhaiya, healing of chronic and acute disease (Narasimha is invoked as the healer of mental illness), success in legal proceedings, restoration of lost wealth, and protection from accidents and untimely death. The phalashruti specifically notes that wearing the sahasranama as a kavach-amulet inscribed on bhurja-patra protects pregnant women, small children and those undergoing serious medical treatment. Spiritual rewards include bhakti-vairagya, purification of consciousness, and ultimately moksha in Vaikuntha. (Sources: Garuda Purana; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Narasimha worship particularly powerful for protection?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha holds a unique place in Vaishnava theology as the avatar of Vishnu specifically incarnated for protection — his entire purpose was to protect the devotee Prahlada from his demonic father. Unlike avatars who teach (Krishna, Rama) or restore cosmic balance (Varaha, Vamana), Narasimha's defining act is direct intervention to save a bhakta in mortal danger. This makes his stotras and especially his sahasranama and kavacham the most respected protective recitations in Vaishnava tradition. The fierce form is not malevolent but protective: directed entirely against the enemies of the devotee, never against the devotee himself. Acharyas across sampradayas have composed Narasimha stotras, and Madhva Vaishnavism in particular places enormous devotional weight on Lakshmi-Narasimha worship. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7; Hindupedia Narasimha; Wikipedia Madhva.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Narasimha Sahasranama different from the Narasimha Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are major Narasimha stotras but serve different functions. The Narasimha Kavacham, attributed to sage Prahlada and preserved in the Brahmanda Purana, is an armor invocation in which each verse appoints a specific form of Narasimha to guard a specific limb or direction — Ugra-Narasimha at the head, Vajra-nakha at the neck, Lakshmi-Narasimha at the heart and so on. It takes 8-12 minutes to recite. The Narasimha Sahasranama is the 1000-name compendium, broader in scope and focused on praise rather than limb-by-limb protection. Devotees often combine both: the kavacham for daily protective recitation and the sahasranama on Saturdays, Narasimha Jayanti and during Sade Sati periods. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Wikipedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is the principal Narasimha pilgrimage centre?
seoFaqs.answer: Ahobilam in Andhra Pradesh's Nallamala forest range is the supreme Narasimha kshetra, comprising nine separate Narasimha shrines (Nava-Narasimha) spread across the hills — Bhargava Narasimha, Yogananda Narasimha, Chatravata Narasimha, Ahobila Narasimha (the central shrine), Varaha Narasimha, Malola Narasimha, Jvala Narasimha (where Narasimha is said to have killed Hiranyakashipu), Pavana Narasimha and Karancha Narasimha. The Ahobilam Mutt is one of the principal Sri Vaishnava monasteries. Other major Narasimha temples include Simhachalam in Visakhapatnam (the famous Varaha-Lakshmi-Narasimha shrine), Mangalagiri in Guntur, the Yadagirigutta temple in Telangana, and the Narasimha shrines at Penna Ahobilam and Sholingur. (Sources: Wikipedia Ahobilam; Hindupedia Narasimha; Drikpanchang.)
